assertions.expectation: Explains that multi-host TPU slices are recreated atomically in a single step — not a rolling upgrade
assertions.reason: The response mentions slices will be terminated and recreated but doesn't explicitly state this happens atomically in a single step rather than as a rolling upgrade

assertions.expectation: Warns that a maintenance event on ANY single slice triggers restart of ALL slices in the multislice environment
assertions.reason: The response doesn't explicitly warn that maintenance on any single slice affects all slices in the multislice setup

assertions.expectation: Notes that all pods on all affected slices will restart simultaneously — the training job will be interrupted
assertions.reason: The response clearly states that training processes will be interrupted and TPU slices will be terminated

assertions.expectation: Explains that if insufficient TPU resources are available during recreation, the slice blocks until hardware is freed — potentially causing extended downtime
assertions.reason: The response mentions downtime can range from 15 minutes to several hours but doesn't explain the specific issue of resource availability blocking recreation

assertions.expectation: Recommends saving a training checkpoint immediately before the maintenance window
assertions.reason: The response explicitly recommends triggering a manual checkpoint right before the maintenance window

assertions.expectation: Recommends configuring the training framework for automatic checkpoint resume so it can restart after the maintenance event
assertions.reason: The response includes restart strategy with automated scripts that restore from checkpoints and resume training from the exact step

assertions.expectation: Suggests using maintenance exclusions to defer the event if the training run is close to completion
assertions.reason: The response doesn't mention maintenance exclusions as an option to defer the event

assertions.expectation: Mentions maintenance windows to control when the event occurs — schedule it during a planned break in training
assertions.reason: While the response mentions coordinating with natural training breakpoints, it doesn't specifically discuss using maintenance windows to control timing
